steps.title: Identify the y-intercept and slope direction
steps.content: The line crosses the y-axis at (0, -5). All four answer choices have the constant term -5, confirming the y-intercept. Since the line descends from left to right, the slope must be negative. This immediately eliminates choices A and B, which both have positive slopes.

steps.title: Test the remaining choices using a marked point
steps.content:
The graph shows the point (7, -8) on the line.

For choice C: $f(7) = -\frac{3}{7}(7) - 5 = -3 - 5 = -8$

For choice D: $f(7) = -\frac{7}{3}(7) - 5 = -\frac{49}{3} - 5 \approx -21.3$

Only choice C produces the correct y-value. That matches choice C — C is correct.
